subject: Social Science (Civics)
overall_learning_objectives: Know the structure of the Indian judiciary
Learn the role of courts in delivering justice
Understand the importance of independence of the judiciary
Identify different levels of courts in India
Recognize how courts protect the rights of citizens

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Judiciary
student_activities: Students discuss why rules and justice are important
Share examples where courts solve disputes

lesson_plan.title: Structure of the Indian Judiciary
student_activities: Draw a simple diagram showing three levels of courts
List the names of the courts

lesson_plan.title: Role of the Courts
student_activities: Discuss a simple dispute and how a court might solve it

lesson_plan.title: Independence of the Judiciary
student_activities: Discuss why judges should be fair and unbiased
Write one reason why judicial independence is important

lesson_plan.title: Types of Cases and Public Interest Litigation
student_activities: Identify examples of civil and criminal cases

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Chapter Summary
student_activities: Write two ways courts protect people

teacher_tip: Use simple real-life examples of disputes to help students understand how courts provide justice.
